title: cerastium subtriflorum
intro: Cerastium subtriflorum, also known as the Chinese snow-in-summer, is a perennial flowering plant native to China and other parts of Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cerastium subtriflorum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to improve immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Cerastium subtriflorum are typically made from its roots, leaves, and flowers, and can be administered as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
